It was established on 28 November 1959 by [Sultan Omar Ali Saifuddien III](/wiki/Sultan_Omar_Ali_Saifuddien_III "Sultan Omar Ali Saifuddien III").[[2]](#cite_note-:0-2)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

| Sovereign | [Hassanal Bolkiah](/wiki/Hassanal_Bolkiah "Hassanal Bolkiah") |
| Grades | * First Class (PANB) * Second Class (PaNB) |
| Precedence | |
| Next (higher) | [Order of Seri Paduka Mahkota Brunei](/wiki/Order_of_Seri_Paduka_Mahkota_Brunei "Order of Seri Paduka Mahkota Brunei") |

**The Most Faithful Order of Perwira Agong Negara Brunei**[[1]](#cite_note-1) ([Malay](/wiki/Malay_language "Malay language"): *Darjah Perwira Agong Negara Brunei Yang Amat Setia*), also translated as **The Most Faithful Order of Gallantry of Brunei**,[[2]](#cite_note-:0-2) is an [order](/wiki/Order_(decoration) "Order (decoration)") of [Brunei](/wiki/Brunei "Brunei"). It was established on 28 November 1959 by [Sultan Omar Ali Saifuddien III](/wiki/Sultan_Omar_Ali_Saifuddien_III "Sultan Omar Ali Saifuddien III").[[2]](#cite_note-:0-2)

## Current classes

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Order_of_Perwira_Agong_Negara_Brunei&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Current classes")]

The two classes of appointment to the Order are, from highest grade to lowest :

| Class | [Post-nominal](/wiki/Post-nominal_letters "Post-nominal letters") | Ribbon |
| --- | --- | --- |
| First Class | PANB |  |
| Second Class | PaNB |  |

## Recipients

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Order_of_Perwira_Agong_Negara_Brunei&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Recipients")]

### First Class

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Order_of_Perwira_Agong_Negara_Brunei&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: First Class")]

* Unknown – [Hassanal Bolkiah](/wiki/Hassanal_Bolkiah "Hassanal Bolkiah") – [Sultan of Brunei](/wiki/Sultan_of_Brunei "Sultan of Brunei")[[3]](#cite_note-:1-3)
* Unknown – [Omar Ali Saifuddien III](/wiki/Omar_Ali_Saifuddien_III "Omar Ali Saifuddien III") – Sultan of Brunei[[3]](#cite_note-:1-3)
* Unknown – C.E.B. Parrott – Controller of Telecommunications[[4]](#cite_note-:2-4)
* Unknown – Colonel [Jocklin](/wiki/Jocklin_Kongpaw "Jocklin Kongpaw") – Commanding Officer of Air Wing ABDB[[5]](#cite_note-:02-5)
* 1963 – William lan Glass – Controller of Civil Aviation and Establishment Officer[[4]](#cite_note-:2-4)[[6]](#cite_note-6)
* 1963 – [Pengiran Mohammed Abdul Rahman Piut](/wiki/Pengiran_Mohammed_Abdul_Rahman_Piut "Pengiran Mohammed Abdul Rahman Piut") – [Wazir](/wiki/Vizier_(Brunei) "Vizier (Brunei)")[[7]](#cite_note-:5-7)
* 1963 – Mohamed Hassan Maun – Service during the [Brunei Revolt](/wiki/Brunei_revolt "Brunei revolt")[[7]](#cite_note-:5-7)
* 1963 – Besar Kula – Service during the Brunei Revolt[[7]](#cite_note-:5-7)
* 1966 – Lieutenant Colonel [Musa](/wiki/Musa_Yakub "Musa Yakub") – Commander of the Training Institute RBAF[[8]](#cite_note-:3-8)
* 1967 – Major J.R.E. Laird – Capturing [North Kalimantan National Army](/wiki/Brunei_People%27s_Party "Brunei People's Party") rebels [[9]](#cite_note-:4-9)
* 1967 – Major Herbert Marshall – Capturing [North Kalimantan National Army](/wiki/Brunei_People%27s_Party "Brunei People's Party") rebels [[9]](#cite_note-:4-9)

### Second Class

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Order_of_Perwira_Agong_Negara_Brunei&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Second Class")]

* 1963 – Awang Dollah – Service during the Brunei Revolt[[7]](#cite_note-:5-7)
* 1963 – Sable Judah – Service during the Brunei Revolt[[7]](#cite_note-:5-7)
* 1963 – Abdul Hamid Ahmad – Service during the Brunei Revolt[[7]](#cite_note-:5-7)
* 1963 – Abdul Ghani Abdullah – Service during the Brunei Revolt[[7]](#cite_note-:5-7)
* 1963 – Ismail Kalong – Service during the Brunei Revolt[[7]](#cite_note-:5-7)
* 1966 – Major General [Husin](/wiki/Husin_Ahmad "Husin Ahmad") – [Commander of the Royal Brunei Armed Forces](/wiki/Commander_of_the_Royal_Brunei_Armed_Forces "Commander of the Royal Brunei Armed Forces")[[8]](#cite_note-:3-8)
